Bash(1 << 'SSHEOF'
# Check what proxy is running
ps aux | grep -i proxy
echo "---"
# Check proxy config
cat /etc/3proxy/3proxy.cfg 2>/dev/null || cat /etc/3proxy.cfg 2>/dev/null || echo "3proxy config not found"
echo "---"
# Check if squid or other proxy
which squid 2>/dev/null; which 3proxy 2>/dev/null; which tinyproxy 2>/dev/null; which dante 2>/dev/null
echo "---"
# Check listening ports
netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null || ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null
echo "---"
# Check recent connections to the proxy
netstat -tnp 2>/dev/null | grep 8888 | head -20
SSHEOF)

Bash(gh issue create --title "Show detailed file info when selecting from uploads" --body "$\(cat <<''EOF''
## Description

When a user selects a file from `/videos` \(uploads list\), show detailed metadata before offering actions.

## Current behavior

Level 1 menu shows filename, size — basic info only.

## Expected behavior

Display rich metadata:
- **Audio**: duration, bitrate, sample rate, codec, album/artist tags
- **Video**: duration, resolution, fps, codec, bitrate
- **Document**: page count \(if available\), format
- **Image**: dimensions, format, color space

## Context

This is the foundation for file modification features \(speed change, cutting\) — users need to see file details to make informed editing decisions.
EOF
\)" --label "enhancement")

import sys, json
d = json.load\(sys.stdin\)
if d:
    plain = d[0].get\('plainLyrics',''\)
    has_markers = any\(m in plain for m in ['[Verse', '[Chorus', '[Bridge', '[Hook', '[Intro']\)
    print\('Has section markers:', has_markers\)
    # Show first marker if exists
    for line in plain.split\('\\n'\):
        if '[' in line and any\(m in line for m in ['Verse', 'Chorus', 'Bridge']\):
            print\('Marker example:', line\)
            break
" 2>/dev/null)

Bash(mv crates/doracore/tests/smartcrop_integration.rs /tmp/sc_test_bak.rs 2>/dev/null; mv crates/doracore/src/conversion/smartcrop /tmp/sc_mod_bak 2>/dev/null; git add crates/dorabot/Cargo.toml crates/dorabot/src/download/source/ytdlp.rs crates/dorabot/src/telegram/commands.rs crates/dorabot/src/telegram/preview/formats.rs crates/dorabot/src/telegram/preview/metadata.rs crates/doracore/src/download/source/ytdlp.rs && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
fix: support all YouTube video resolutions \(144p–4320p\)

Videos with non-standard resolutions \(e.g. 202x360 = 240p\) were
invisible to the bot because the format parser only recognized
4 hardcoded quality levels \(1080p/720p/480p/360p\).

Replaced exact resolution matching with range-based classification
and explicit quality label detection from yt-dlp output. Now supports
9 quality tiers: 4320p, 2160p, 1440p, 1080p, 720p, 480p, 360p, 240p, 144p.

v0.26.3
EOF
\)"; RESULT=$?; mv /tmp/sc_test_bak.rs crates/doracore/tests/smartcrop_integration.rs 2>/dev/null; mv /tmp/sc_mod_bak crates/doracore/src/conversion/smartcrop 2>/dev/null; exit $RESULT)

fix: support all YouTube video resolutions \(144p–4320p\)

Videos with non-standard resolutions \(e.g. 202x360 = 240p\) were
invisible to the bot because the format parser only recognized
4 hardcoded quality levels \(1080p/720p/480p/360p\).

Replaced exact resolution matching with range-based classification
and explicit quality label detection from yt-dlp output. Now supports
9 quality tiers: 4320p, 2160p, 1440p, 1080p, 720p, 480p, 360p, 240p, 144p.

v0.26.3
EOF
\)")
